title: Edge Bars (Left or Right)
description: Visual indicators on the left or right side of cards that show learning progress or recent sync activity.
content:
### Show learning status

Shows your memorization progress for each card:

* **⚪ Gray** - Disabled card (won't appear in study mode)
* **🟠 Orange** - Forgotten card
* **🟢 Green** - Memorized card

### Show recently synced

Shows which cards changed during the last sync with your Excel or CSV file:

#### From File to App

* **🟠 Bright Orange** - Card was updated from the file
* **🟢 Bright Green** - Card was added from the file

#### From App to File

* **🟠 Dim Orange** - Card was updated from the app
* **🟢 Dim Green** - Card was added from the app

#### Unchanged

* **No color** - Card wasn't modified during sync

Learn more about how sync works in [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards).

### Changing Edge Bar Settings

Configure edge bar visibility in [App Settings](/docs/settings/all-decks#show-left-edge-bar).

title: Cards List - Managing Your Flashcards
description: The Cards List screen is your deck management hub. View all cards at once, search, reorder, edit, delete.
content:
### Opening Cards List From the home screen: 1. **Tap "⋮" on a deck** to open the deck menu. 2. **Select "List Cards"** You'll see all your cards in a scrollable list/grid view. *** ### Card List Display Each card shows: * **#** - Card number, ordinal position * **Term** - front of card * **Definition** - back of card * **Edge Bars** (left or right) - Colored bars indicate card status * **Max lines** - Configurable in [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/)

### Long-Press a Card **Long-Press a Card** select the card and switch to the multi-select mode. title: Multi-Select Cards
description: Select multiple flashcards at once for bulk operations like moving, cutting, pasting, and deleting.
content:
## Entering Multi-Select Mode **Method 1: Card Menu** 1. Tap a card 2. Select **"Select / Cut"** from the menu **Method 2: Long-Press** * Long-press any card to instantly enter multi-select mode ## Working with Selected Cards ### Selecting and Deselecting * **Tap any card** to add it to your selection * **Tap a selected card** again to deselect it * Selected cards will be visually highlighted ### Multi-Select Actions Menu While in multi-select mode, **long-press any card** to open the action menu: * **Select** – Select subsequent cards in the list * **Delete selected** – Permanently remove all selected cards * **Paste** – Move all selected cards to appear after the long-pressed card
### Exiting Multi-Select Mode **Tap the back arrow (←)** in the toolbar to deselect all cards and return to normal browsing mode. ## Important Limitations ⚠️ **Deck-Specific Operations** – Cut and paste operations only work within the same deck. You cannot cut cards from one deck and paste them into a different deck. ## Related Topics * [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) – Overview of card management * [Reordering Cards](/docs/cards-list/reordering/) – Single card repositioning * [Deleting Cards](/docs/cards-list/deleting/) – Single and bulk deletion * [Export/Import Database](/docs/home/database-backup/) – Move entire decks between devices

title: Reordering Cards
description: GoCards supports intuitive drag-and-drop reordering in the Cards List, allowing you to arrange your flashcards in any order you prefer.
content:
## How to Reorder Cards

### Drag-and-Drop Method

1. **Long-press a card** (hold for approximately 1 second)
2. **Drag the card** up or down to your desired position
3. **Release** to drop the card in place
4. The card slides into its new position, and all other cards automatically renumber

### Multi-Select Cut and Paste Method

1. **Long-press a card** (hold for approximately 1 second) to enter multi-select mode
2. **Tap additional cards** to add them to your selection
3. **Long-press the card** after which you want to move your selected cards
4. The multi-select action menu appears
5. **Tap "Paste"**
6. Selected cards are moved to the new location

For more details, see [Multi-Select Operations](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/).

title: Database Backup & Restore
description: Database backups preserve everything all decks, cards, settings, and review history.
content:
## Why Back Up Your Database? * **Complete backup** - Saves all decks, progress, and settings in one file * **Device transfers** - Move your data to a new phone seamlessly * **Safety net** - Protect your data before syncing with Excel/CSV or making major changes *** ## Exporting Your Database **To export a database:** 1. Go to the **home screen** (Recent Decks or All Decks tab) 2. Tap **deck** → **More (⋮)** → **Export DB** 3. Choose save location ### What's Included in the Export The exported `.db` file contains: * ✅ All cards in the deck * ✅ Card content (terms and definitions) * ✅ Learning progress (review intervals, memorization status) * ✅ Deck settings and preferences
*** ## Importing a Database **To import a database:** 1. Go to the **home screen** (Recent Decks or All Decks tab) 2. Tap the **"⋮" menu** (top-right corner) -→ **"Import DB"** 3. Choose the `.db` file from your device 4. Decks appear in All Decks list
*** ## Best Practices ### Recommended Storage Locations **Cloud storage (recommended):** * **Google Drive** - Automatic cloud backup with version history * **Dropbox** - Cross-device sync and easy sharing * **OneDrive** - Integrated with Microsoft accounts **Local storage:** * **Downloads folder** - Then manually transfer to your computer * **Email to yourself** - Quick option for small databases *** ## Database vs. Excel/CSV Export Choose the right export format for your needs: | Feature | Database Export (.db) | Deck Export (Excel/CSV) | |---------|----------------------|-------------------------| | **All cards** | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | | **Learning progress** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Review intervals** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Deck settings** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Term/Definition ratio** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Font size settings** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Editable on computer** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | | **Sync with file** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | ### When to Use Database Export * Creating complete backups with all progress * Transferring data to a new device * Taking snapshots before major changes * Preserving your learning history ### When to Use Excel/CSV Export * Editing cards in Excel or Google Sheets * Sharing deck content with others (without progress) * Syncing cards between devices using files * Bulk editing card content *** ## Related Topics * [Import Cards from Excel/CSV](/docs/sync-export-import/import-cards/) - Add cards from spreadsheets * [Export Cards to Excel/CSV](/docs/sync-export-import/export-cards/) - Save cards for editing * [Sync Cards with Files](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Keep cards in sync with Excel/CSV

title: 📁 All Decks
description: Your complete deck library with folder organization. Navigate through folders and subfolders to find any deck.
content:
## Topbar Actions On the topbar, you can see the following actions: * **🔍 [Search](deck-list/search-decks)** - Find decks by name across all folders * **"+" button** to [create a new deck](deck-list/create-folder) * Tap "⋮" in the topbar in the top-right to open the menu with these options: * [New Folder](deck-list/create-folder) * [Import Excel](sync-export-import/import-cards) * [Import CSV](sync-export-import/import-cards) * [Import DB](deck-list/database-backup) * [Settings](settings/app)
*** ## Deck Menu Actions **Tap "⋮" on the deck** to open the menu with these options: * [**Browse**](browse-cards) (Premium) - View-only carousel (no grading) * [**List Cards**](cards-list) - See all cards in a list * **Add a new card** - Create a new card quickly at the end * [**Cut**](cards-list/reorder-cards) - Move deck to a different folder * **Rename** - Change the deck name * [**Delete**](cards-list/delete-cards) - Remove deck and all its cards (with confirmation)

title: Adding Cards
description: Learn how to create new flashcards in GoCards from the Cards List, Study Mode, or Edit Card screen.
content:
## How to Add a New Card You can add cards from several places in the app: ### From Cards List **Method 1: Add at the end of deck** 1. Open your deck's Cards List (tap "⋮" on a deck → "List Cards") 2. Tap the **"+" button** in the top toolbar 3. Enter your card content and tap **Save** **Method 2: Insert at a specific position** 1. In Cards List, tap any card to open its menu 2. Select **"Add a new card here"** 3. The new card will be inserted after the selected card ### From Study Mode or Edit Card Screen 1. Tap the **"⋮" menu** in the top toolbar 2. Select **"New card"** 3. Enter your content and save *** ## Card Fields When creating a new card, you'll see two main text fields: | Field | Description | |-------|-------------| | **Term** (Front) | The question, word, or prompt you want to memorize | | **Definition** (Back) | The answer, meaning, or information to recall | Both fields support multiple lines and [HTML formatting](/docs/edit-card/html-tags/) for rich content like bold text, images, and links. *** ## Toolbar Actions While adding a card, the toolbar provides: * **💾 Save** - Save the new card to your deck * **🗑️ Delete** - Discard the card without saving * **⋮ More menu:** * **New card** - Save current card and start another new card
*** ## Tips for Creating Cards * **Keep it simple** - One concept per card works best for memorization * **Use both sides** - Put the prompt on the front, the answer on the back * **Add context** - Include example sentences for vocabulary cards * **Format when needed** - Use [HTML tags](/docs/edit-card/html-tags/) for emphasis or images *** ## Related Topics * [HTML Tags](/docs/edit-card/html-tags/) - Format cards with bold, images, and more * [Editing Cards](/docs/edit-card/) - Modify existing cards * [Deleting Cards](/docs/edit-card/delete-card/) - Remove unwanted cards * [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) - Manage all cards in a deck

title: Code Syntax Highlighting
description: Display syntax-highlighted code on your flashcards with automatic language detection. Perfect for programming flashcards, algorithm studies, and technical learning.
content:
GoCards includes built-in syntax highlighting for code blocks on your flashcards using [Highlight.js](https://highlightjs.org/), making it perfect for learning programming languages, algorithms, APIs, and technical concepts.
*** ## How to Add Code to Cards To display syntax-highlighted code on your flashcard, wrap your code in `
` HTML tags:

```html

function isPrime(n) {
  if (n <= 1) return false;
  for (let i = 2; i * i <= n; i++) {
    if (n % i === 0) return false;
  }
  return true;
}

```

**Important:** Use both tags together: `
` at the start and `
` at the end. This ensures proper formatting and syntax highlighting.

***

## Automatic Language Detection

GoCards automatically detects which programming language you're using - **no language specification needed!**

### About Highlight.js

The app uses [Highlight.js](https://highlightjs.org/), the Internet's favorite JavaScript syntax highlighter.

**Key Features:**

* **192 programming languages** supported with automatic detection
* **No configuration required** - works with standard `
` HTML tags
* **Smart language detection** - analyzes code structure to identify the language
* **Compatible with any framework** - pure JavaScript with no dependencies
* **Optimized performance** - only loads when code blocks are present

### Supported Languages

Highlight.js recognizes **192 programming languages** including:

* **Popular languages:** JavaScript, Python, Java, Kotlin, C++, C#, Go, Rust, Ruby, PHP, Swift, TypeScript
* **Web technologies:** HTML, CSS, XML, JSON, YAML
* **Scripts & shells:** Bash, PowerShell, Shell
* **Databases:** SQL, PostgreSQL, MySQL
* **And many more...**

The syntax highlighter analyzes your code's structure and automatically applies the appropriate color scheme.

***

## Related Topics

* [HTML Tags](/docs/edit-card/html-tags/) - Format cards with bold, images, lists, and more
* [Adding Cards](/docs/edit-card/add-card/) - Create new flashcards
* [Editing Cards](/docs/edit-card/) - Modify your flashcard content
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Edit code in Excel/CSV and sync back (Premium)

title: Deleting Cards
description: Remove unwanted flashcards from your decks. Delete from Cards List, Study Mode, or Edit Mode.
content:
## How to Delete a Card GoCards provides multiple ways to delete cards, all with **undo support** so you never lose a card by accident. ### From Study Mode 1. While studying, tap the **🗑️ Delete** icon in the toolbar 2. The card is removed and the next card appears 3. Tap **"Restore"** in the snackbar or press **Back** to undo ### From Edit Mode 1. While editing a card, tap the **🗑️ Delete** icon 2. Card is removed immediately 3. Use the snackbar or **Back** button to restore ### From Cards List **Method 1: Popup Menu** 1. Tap any card to open its menu 2. Select **"Delete"** 3. Card is removed with a snackbar showing **"Restore"** option
**Method 2: Swipe to Delete** 1. Swipe any card **left or right** 2. Card is deleted instantly 3. Tap **"Restore"** in the snackbar to undo **Method 3: Bulk Delete** 1. Long-press a card to enter selection mode 2. Tap additional cards to select them 3. Tap the **Delete** icon in the toolbar 4. All selected cards are removed with undo option
*** ## Undo Options GoCards uses an **undo-first approach** instead of confirmation dialogs, making deletion quick while still safe: | Method | How to Undo | |--------|-------------| | **Snackbar** | Tap "Restore" in the message that appears after deletion | | **Back button** | Press back immediately after deleting (Study/Edit mode) | **Note:** The undo option is available for a short time after deletion. Act quickly if you need to restore a card. *** ## Tips * **Swipe is fastest** - In Cards List, swipe left or right for quick deletion * **Bulk delete** - Long-press to select multiple cards, then delete all at once *** ## Related Topics * [Adding Cards](/docs/edit-card/add-card/) - Create new flashcards * [Editing Cards](/docs/edit-card/) - Modify existing cards * [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) - Manage all cards in a deck * [Multi-Select](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/) - Select and manage multiple cards

title: Getting Started with GoCards
description: Welcome to GoCards! This guide will walk you through creating your first deck and starting your learning journey.
content:
## Your First Time Opening GoCards Let's start in the **All Decks** tab.
## Step 1: Create Your First Deck [Decks](/deck-list) are collections of flashcards organized by topic. Let's create your first one: 1. **Tap the "All Decks" tab** at the bottom 2. **Tap the "+" button** in the bottom-right corner 3. **Enter a name** for your deck (e.g., "Spanish Vocabulary", "Biology Terms") 4. **Tap "Create"**
Your new deck now appears in the list! ## Step 2: Open the Cards List The [cards list](/cards-list) is where you'll manage all the flashcards in your deck: 1. **Tap "⋮" on a deck** to open the deck menu. 2. **Select "List Cards"**
You'll see an empty list since we haven't added any cards yet. Let's fix that! ## Step 3: Add Your First Cards Now let's [add some cards](/edit-card/add-card) to your deck: 1. **Tap the "+" button** in the top-right corner 2. **Enter the Term** (the question - front of the card) 3. **Enter the Definition** (the answer - back of the card) 4. **Tap the save icon** (💾) to [save your card](/edit-card)
Repeat to add more cards. Later, you can enhance cards with [HTML formatting](/edit-card/html-tags), images, or even YouTube videos. ## Step 4: Study Your Cards Now it's time to start your [study session](/study)! 1. **Go back** to your deck (tap the back arrow) 2. **Tap on the deck** to enter study mode
### Your First Study Session In study mode, you'll see: * **Term** at the top (the question) * **Tap the bottom section** to reveal the definition (answer) * **Swipe left or right** to navigate between cards After revealing the answer, grade yourself based on how well you remembered: | Button | When to Use | Next Review | |--------|-------------|-------------| | **Again** (Red) | You forgot | Soon (stays in current session) | | **Quick** (Orange) | For cramming | 5 minutes | | **Hard** (Blue) | Remembered with difficulty | 3 days | | **Easy** (Green) | Remembered easily | 5 days | Each button shows when you'll see the card again. This is spaced repetition—the app schedules reviews right before you're likely to forget, making learning more efficient. If you prefer to review cards without affecting your study schedule, try [Browse Mode](/browse-cards). *** ## Next Steps You've created your first deck and learned the basics! As you continue, you'll want to explore: **Edit cards more efficiently:** Instead of editing cards one-by-one on your phone, you can [sync with Excel or CSV files](/sync-export-import/sync-cards) on your computer. Make bulk changes with find-replace, then sync back—without losing your study progress. **Create richer cards:** Go beyond plain text with [HTML formatting](/edit-card/html-tags)—add bold text, images, YouTube videos, and code syntax highlighting to make your cards more engaging. Happy studying! 🎓

title: What is GoCards?
description: GoCards is a spaced repetition flashcard app for Android with a unique feature edit your flashcards in Excel/CSV files on your computer, sync them back to the app, and never lose your study progress.
content:
## Key Benefits

### ⚡ Intuitive Gesture Interface

* **Swipe** to navigate and delete cards
* **Pinch** to zoom content
* **Drag & drop** to reorder cards
* **Edge bars** show learning status and sync changes

### 🎨 Rich Content Support

HTML formatting, embedded YouTube videos, images, and bold text. Create engaging, multimedia flashcards that go beyond plain text.

### 💻 Code Syntax Highlighting

Perfect for developers: `<pre><code>` blocks support syntax highlighting for technical flashcards and programming study.

### 🔄 Bi-directional Sync with Excel/CSV

Edit flashcards on your computer using **Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc**, or any CSV editor. Changes sync both ways—edit in the app or on your computer, everything stays in harmony.

### 📊 Never Lose Learning Progress

Your due dates, mastery levels, and learning statistics survive every sync. Update content without resetting your progress—the core feature that sets GoCards apart.

### 🖥️ Edit on Computer

Use **Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc**, or any spreadsheet software to edit your flashcards. Faster typing on a full keyboard, better organization with spreadsheet tools—much more efficient than editing on your phone.

### 🖥️ Bulk Editing Made Easy

Find-replace across hundreds of cards, copy-paste entire sections, and use spreadsheet formulas. Edit hundreds of cards in minutes instead of hours.

### ☁️ Cloud Storage Integration

Save your synced file to Google Drive, Dropbox, or OneDrive. Edit on your laptop, save, and changes appear on your phone automatically. No manual transfers.

### 🤝 Easy Sharing & Backup

Share decks as standard Excel/CSV files—no proprietary formats. Your content is always readable and backed up outside the app.

### 🎯 Spaced Repetition Learning

Smart scheduling shows you cards exactly when you're about to forget them, optimizing retention and minimizing study time.

### 🚫 No Ads

Clean, distraction-free learning experience. Focus on studying, not avoiding pop-ups.

***

## How GoCards is Different from Other Flashcard Apps

| Feature | GoCards | Other Apps |
|---------|---------|------------------|
| **⚡ Gesture-based interface** | ✅ Swipe, pinch, drag - intuitive | ⚠️ Menu-heavy interfaces |
| **🎨 Rich content** | ✅ HTML, YouTube, images | ⚠️ Limited formatting |
| **💻 Code highlighting** | ✅ Built-in syntax highlighting | ❌ Not available |
| **🔄 Bi-directional sync** | ✅ Full Excel/CSV sync both ways | ❌ Import only (one-way) |
| **📊 Preserve study progress** | ✅ Always preserved during sync | ❌ Lost on re-import |
| **🖥️ Edit on computer** | ✅ Use Excel, Google Sheets, any spreadsheet | ⚠️ Limited |
| **🖥️ Bulk editing** | ✅ Find-replace, copy-paste in spreadsheets | ❌ One card at a time in app |
| **☁️ Cloud storage** | ✅ Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive | ⚠️ Proprietary cloud sync |
| **🤝 Shareable format** | ✅ Standard Excel/CSV files | ⚠️ Proprietary formats |
| **🎯 Spaced repetition** | ✅ Smart scheduling algorithm | ✅ Most apps support this |
| **🚫 No ads** | ✅ Clean experience | ⚠️ Often ad-supported |

***

## See It in Action

<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/vp0MnQeySlo?si=QhQDOGJ2anKaqTQV" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

***

## Getting Started

Ready to start learning smarter?

1. **[Download GoCards](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=pl.gocards.app)** from the Google Play Store
2. **[Create your first deck](/getting-started/)** and add some cards
3. **[Learn about sync](/sync-export-import/sync-cards/)** to unlock the full power of GoCards

Have questions? [Join our community](https://discord.gg/weWjDwYz) for support.

title: Study Mode - Learning with Spaced Repetition
description: Master your flashcards with GoCards' study mode featuring spaced repetition algorithm, four grading buttons, interactive gestures, and progress tracking.
content:
## The Four Grading Buttons

After revealing the answer, grade how well you remembered:

### 🔴 Again (Red)

**Use when:** You forgot the card completely

**What happens:**

* Card stays in the current study session
* Reappears after you've studied/forgotten 5 more cards
* Next review: **2 days** (or current interval if already learned)
* Marks card as "forgotten" (visible in card list edge bar)

**Customization:** Change the "forgotten card limit" in [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/)

***

### 🟠 Quick Repetition (Orange)

**Use when:** Cramming for an exam or need short-term recall

**What happens:**

* First review: **5 minutes**
* Second review: **10 minutes**
* Third review: **20 minutes**
* Intervals keep doubling

**Perfect for:**

* Last-minute studying before exams
* Memorizing information needed in the next few hours
* Intensive drilling of difficult material

***

### 🔵 Hard (Blue)

**Use when:** You remembered, but it took effort

**What happens:**

If this is your **first time** grading the card:

* Next review: **3 days**
* Subsequent reviews: **interval × 1.5**

If you previously clicked **"Again"**:

* Updates the failed attempt with **interval × 0.5**
* Creates new review with **interval × 1.5**

**Example progression:**

1. First: 3 days
2. Second: 4.5 days (3 × 1.5)
3. Third: 6.75 days (4.5 × 1.5)

***

### 🟢 Easy (Green)

**Use when:** You remembered quickly and confidently

**What happens:**

If this is your **first time** grading the card:

* Next review: **5 days**
* Subsequent reviews: **interval × 2**

If you previously clicked **"Again"**:

* Updates the failed attempt with **interval × 0.5**
* Marks card as memorized

**Example progression:**

1. First: 5 days
2. Second: 10 days (5 × 2)
3. Third: 20 days (10 × 2)
4. Fourth: 40 days (20 × 2)

***

## Understanding Review Intervals

The interval shown below each button is **when you'll see this card again**.

### How Intervals Work

**New cards** (never studied):

* Again: 2 days
* Quick: 5 minutes
* Hard: 3 days
* Easy: 5 days

**Previously studied cards:**

* Intervals adapt based on your history
* More "Easy" clicks = longer intervals
* "Again" clicks = shorter intervals
* The algorithm balances memory retention with study efficiency

### Why These Intervals?

GoCards uses **spaced repetition** based on memory research:

* Review just before you're about to forget
* Longer intervals strengthen long-term memory
* Too frequent = waste of time
* Too infrequent = you'll forget

## Related Topics

* [Browse Mode](/docs/browse/) - View cards without studying
* [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) - See all cards and their status
* [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/) - Customize study behavior
* [Tips & Best Practices](/docs/support/tips/) - Optimize your learning

title: Undo last action
content:
The app offers two ← Back buttons, each with its own function:

### 1. Exit current activity (top bar)

* Tap **←** in the top bar to close the current screen and return to the **Deck List**

### 2. Undo last action (bottom navigation bar)

* Tap **←** in the bottom navigation bar to undo your most recent action

### What can be undone?

When you tap **←** in the bottom navigation bar, you can:

* **Undo grading** – Go back to the previous card after selecting **Easy, Good, Hard, or Again**
* **Restore a deleted card** – Recover a card you just removed
* **Undo adding a new card** – Remove a newly added card that hasn’t been saved yet

title: Sync Cards with Excel/CSV Files
description: Keep your flashcard decks synchronized with Excel or CSV files stored in cloud storage. Edit your cards on a laptop, save to Google Drive or Dropbox, and changes automatically sync to your phone—all while preserving your study progress.
content:
## Why Sync Instead of Import?

**The Problem with Traditional Apps:**

Most flashcard apps only support importing files. Every time you update your flashcards, you have to delete the old deck and import a new one, losing all your study progress in the process.

**The GoCards Solution:**

Sync your deck once, then edit the file anytime. Changes flow automatically in both directions while your learning statistics, due dates, and mastery levels stay intact.

***

## How Sync Works

GoCards uses smart synchronization that compares modification dates to determine which source has the newest changes.

### Intelligent Card Matching

When syncing, GoCards automatically detects whether a card has been modified or is entirely new:

* **Modified cards** - Updated if there's at least 70% similarity in the term or definition (prevents accidental overwrites)
* **New cards** - Added automatically if similarity is below 70%
* **Deleted cards** - Removed if they no longer exist in the source

This smart matching ensures your edits are preserved while preventing duplicate cards.

### What Gets Synced

**When the file is newer than the deck:**

* New cards from the file are added
* Deleted cards are removed
* Modified cards are updated
* Card order matches the file
* Study progress is preserved

**When the card is newer than the file:**

* Changes from the card are written back to the file
* Custom columns in your spreadsheet are preserved and move with their cards

***

## See It in Action

Watch how sync works in practice:

<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/rthjtSAq13A?si=ipRAJYeRNYG1ermM" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

**What happens in this demo:**

1. A deck is exported to create a synced file
2. Changes are made to both the file (3 cards) and the app (3 cards)
3. Sync is triggered
4. The card list shows what changed with color indicators
5. Study progress remains intact despite the updates

***

## Visual Sync Indicators

After syncing, colored edge bars in your card list show exactly which cards changed.

Learn more in [Edge Bars](/docs/cards-list/edge-bars).

***

## Key Benefits

✅ **Preserve Study Progress** - Never lose your learning statistics again

✅ **Bi-directional Sync** - Changes flow both ways automatically

✅ **Edit Anywhere** - Update cards on laptop or phone

✅ **Cloud Storage Integration** - Works with Google Drive, Dropbox, and more

✅ **Smart Conflict Resolution** - Prevents accidental data loss with similarity checking

✅ **Extra Data Preserved** - Keep custom columns in your spreadsheet intact

***

## Supported File Formats

**Excel (.xlsx, .xls)**

* Compatible with Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc

**CSV (.csv)**

* Universal compatibility with any text editor or spreadsheet program

See the [File Format Guide](/docs/sync-export-import/file-format/) for detailed specifications.

title: Why Use Excel/CSV with GoCards?
description: Excel and CSV files give you powerful ways to work with your flashcards outside the GoCards app. Create cards faster, edit in bulk, share with others, and maintain readable backups.
content:
## Benefits of Using Excel/CSV
